name: Fuente del Caño and Sierra del Moral Loop
name_original: Fuente del Caño y Sierra del Moral — circular desde Moral de Calatrava
description: Hike the moderate 10.5-mile Fuente del Caño and Sierra del Moral Loop, exploring diverse landscapes and the unique Fuente del Caño.
region_intro: Hiking around Moral De Calatrava offers diverse landscapes within the Campo de Calatrava region, characterized by the Sierra del Moral mountain range and the Jabalón River. The area features picturesque quartzite rock formations and low mountain scrub, forming part of the Community Importance Site of the Volcanic Lagoons of Campo de Calatrava. This varied terrain provides a range of natural environments for outdoor exploration. Best hiking trails around Moral De Calatrava The most popular hiking route is Fuente del Caño and Sierra del Moral Loop, a 10.5 miles (16.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 41 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the unique Fuente del Caño and the Sierra del Moral. Another top favourite among local hikers is Oreto y Zuqueca Archaeological Site Loop, a moderate 10.1 miles (16.3 km) path. This trail includes historical sites and offers views of the surrounding landscape. Local hikers also love the Senda de los Colmenares Loop, an 8.2 miles (13.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 39 minutes. Hiking around Moral De Calatrava is defined by its volcanic lagoons, quartzite rock formations, and the presence of the Jabalón River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Moral De Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 70 hikers have used komoot to explore Moral De Calatrava's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Moral De Calatrava?
A: There are nearly 30 hiking routes in the Moral De Calatrava area, offering a variety of experiences. You'll find options ranging from easy strolls to more challenging treks, with 11 easy, 17 moderate, and 1 difficult trail to explore.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Moral De Calatrava?
A: Hiking in Moral De Calatrava offers diverse landscapes, including the picturesque quartzite rock formations of the Sierra del Moral and the varied terrain along the Jabalón River. The region is also part of the Community Importance Site of the Volcanic Lagoons of Campo de Calatrava, providing unique natural environments. In spring, the low mountain scrub fills the air with gentle scents.

Q: Are there any circular hiking trails in Moral De Calatrava?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Moral De Calatrava are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1200094358/fuente-del-cano-and-sierra-del-moral-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Caño and Sierra del Moral Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which explores diverse landscapes, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1183503474/oreto-y-zuqueca-archaeological-site-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oreto y Zuqueca Archaeological Site Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which combines nature with historical sites.

Q: What are some interesting historical or natural landmarks to see near the trails?
A: The area around Moral De Calatrava is rich in both natural and historical landmarks. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2713622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oreto and Zuqueca Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt;, or explore natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4573401" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Caño Cave&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features several volcanic formations such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1789437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Gordo Volcano&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6832426" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Yezosa Volcano&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Moral De Calatrava?
A: Spring is an excellent time for hiking in Moral De Calatrava. During this season, the low mountain scrub is fragrant, and you might even find wild asparagus after rain in certain areas. The weather is generally pleasant for exploring the trails and enjoying the natural beauty.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of protected wildlife?
A: The Sierra del Moral, which borders Moral De Calatrava, is recognized as an important area for various protected species. While hiking, you might have the chance to spot birds like the Iberian imperial eagle and black vulture, or even the elusive Iberian lynx, as the area forms part of the Community Importance Site of the Volcanic Lagoons of Campo de Calatrava.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Moral De Calatrava?
A: The trails in Moral De Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the unique volcanic landscapes, and the opportunities to explore both natural beauty and historical sites.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes available?
A: For those seeking a more challenging experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1201414604/fuente-del-cano-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Caño Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route spanning over 30 kilometers and typically taking around 8 hours to complete. It offers an extensive exploration of the region's diverse terrain.

Q: Can I find trails that pass by water features?
A: Yes, the region is bathed by the Jabalón River, and some trails will take you near water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1200094358/fuente-del-cano-and-sierra-del-moral-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Caño and Sierra del Moral Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, explores areas with unique natural elements, including the Fuente del Caño itself.

Q: Are there any trails suitable for a moderate day hike?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Moral De Calatrava are moderate and perfect for a day hike. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1200045906/senda-de-los-colmenares-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda de los Colmenares Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option, covering about 13 kilometers and typically completed in under 4 hours, leading through varied terrain.

Q: What kind of geological features can be observed on the hikes?
A: The hiking trails around Moral De Calatrava offer excellent opportunities to observe unique geological features. You'll encounter beautiful quartzite rock formations, particularly within the Sierra del Moral, and the area is also known for its volcanic lagoons, providing a distinct geological landscape.

Q: Are there any trails that include archaeological sites?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1183503474/oreto-y-zuqueca-archaeological-site-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oreto y Zuqueca Archaeological Site Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate trail that specifically includes historical sites, allowing you to combine your hike with a visit to significant cultural landmarks in the region.
tips:
Impressive grotto through which water emanates naturally and from which the Arroyo del Caño starts. An official hiking route starts from Bolaños de Cva., along the arranged road to the Virgen del Monte, and from there it continues to the Sierra de Moral. From Bolaños there are 30km there and back, and 20km if you do it from the Virgen del Monte (approximately). It is a somewhat long walk to walk, so I have done it by bike but knowing that the paths that go up to the Caño are narrow, with stones and quite steep. They cannot be done by bike, but I think it is a good option to do the route from Bolaños by bike if you assume that you will have to pick it up a few times and walk with it for a few kilometers along the crest of the mountain range. What an adventure 😅

At the mouth of the cave you can see Bolaños and Almagro.
In summer there is only a puddle of water, but it is spectacular. I suppose that in spring it will take a little more water.

The Sierra del Moral is a small mountain system that is located to the north of the city of Moral de Calatrava and at its top there are a set of windmills from where you can see the views of the town.

The hermitage is located on the Paseo de San Roque, at the foot of the San Cristóbal hill. It was built at the end of the 18th century to replace another primitive located in another place. It is very similar to the hermitage of the Virgen de la Sierra.

faqs.question: What is the estimated hiking time for this loop?
faqs.answer: The Fuente del Caño and Sierra del Moral Loop is a moderate hike that typically takes around 4 hours and 41 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The route offers a diverse landscape, blending flat Manchegan plains with limestone and clay soil, and the more rugged elevations of the Sierra del Moral. You'll encounter unique geological features, including volcanic formations like Las Herrerías and La Yezosa, as well as narrow, stony, and sometimes steep paths, particularly leading up to the Caño.

faqs.question: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the way, you can explore the Fuente del Caño Cave, a natural water source high in the mountains. You'll also pass by Molinos de Moral and the historic San Roque Hermitage (Moral de Calatrava). The trail also offers views of the Sierra del Moral, El Pardillo, and Sierra Pelada mountain ranges, and passes near the Sanctuary of the Virgen del Monte.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While parts of the trail are relatively flat, the overall difficulty is rated as moderate. Sections leading to the Fuente del Caño can be narrow, stony, and quite steep, which might be challenging for absolute beginners. Hikers should have a reasonable level of fitness.

faqs.question: What wildlife and plants can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The area is rich in biodiversity. You might spot wildlife such as deer, wild boar, rabbits, hares, red partridges, and quail. The vegetation includes diverse flora like mosses, scrub oaks, thyme, rosemary, gorse, low scrubland, and oak landscapes.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with milder temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summers can be very hot in this region, and some paths might be muddy after heavy rains.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While the specific starting point for this loop can vary, common access points are often found near Moral de Calatrava or Bolaños. For the 'Ruta de la Fuente del Caño' specifically, starting from Moral de Calatrava is a good option. Parking availability will depend on your chosen starting point within these municipalities.

faqs.question: Is the Fuente del Caño and Sierra del Moral Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, given the presence of wildlife and livestock farming in the rural environment, it is advisable to keep dogs on a leash to ensure their safety and respect the local ecosystem.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Fuente del Caño and Sierra del Moral Loop. It is a publicly accessible trail.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Ruta de la Fuente del Caño and the Motilla del Azuer - Moral de Calatrava trail.

faqs.question: Is there water available on the trail?
faqs.answer: The Fuente del Caño itself is a natural water source, known for water flowing almost year-round from its caves. However, it's always recommended to carry sufficient water for your hike, as the potability of natural sources can vary and access might not always be convenient.
